beginner.question: What is a CSS navigation bar?
beginner.answer: A CSS navigation bar is a styled set of links, typically using an unordered list, that allows users to navigate a website.

beginner.question: How do you create a horizontal navigation bar?
beginner.answer: Set li { display: inline-block; } or li { float: left; } and style the anchor tags as block elements.

beginner.question: How do you create a vertical navigation bar?
beginner.answer: Set li { display: block; } or display: block on anchor tags inside the list items stacked vertically.

beginner.question: Why are unordered lists commonly used for navigation bars?
beginner.answer: Unordered lists provide semantic structure for lists of links, improving accessibility and SEO.

beginner.question: What CSS properties are used to style navigation links?
beginner.answer: Common properties include color, background-color, padding, text-decoration, display: block, and font-weight.

beginner.question: How do you remove bullet points from a navigation list?
beginner.answer: Use list-style: none on the ul element to remove bullet points.

beginner.question: How do you make navigation links fill the full width of the nav bar?
beginner.answer: Set display: block on the anchor tags and width: 100% on the list items.

beginner.question: What is a responsive hamburger menu?
beginner.answer: A hamburger menu is a collapsible navigation icon (three horizontal lines) that reveals links on small screens.

beginner.question: How do you change the background color of a navigation bar?
beginner.answer: Set background-color on the nav or ul element to apply a background color to the entire navigation.

beginner.question: How do you highlight the current page in navigation?
beginner.answer: Add an active class (e.g., class='active') to the current page link and style it with a distinct color or background.

intermediate.question: How do you create a sticky navigation bar?
intermediate.answer: Use position: sticky; top: 0; on the nav element to make it stick at the top when scrolling.

intermediate.question: How do you create a fixed navigation bar?
intermediate.answer: Use position: fixed; top: 0; width: 100%; on the nav element to keep it at the top of the viewport.

intermediate.question: How do you add hover effects to navigation links?
intermediate.answer: Use a:hover { background-color: ...; color: ...; } to change link appearance on hover.

intermediate.question: How do you build a responsive navigation with media queries?
intermediate.answer: Use @media (max-width: 768px) to switch from horizontal to vertical layout or show/hide the hamburger menu.

intermediate.question: How do you create a full-width navigation bar?
intermediate.answer: Set nav { width: 100%; } and remove any padding/margin defaults on body and ul.

intermediate.question: How do you style dropdown items in a navigation bar?
intermediate.answer: Use nested unordered lists with absolute positioning and visibility toggle on hover for dropdown submenus.

intermediate.question: How do you add icons between navigation links?
intermediate.answer: Add icons using ::before pseudo-elements or include icon markup inside the anchor tags.

intermediate.question: How do you distribute navigation items evenly?
intermediate.answer: Use Flexbox with justify-content: space-around or space-between on the ul.

intermediate.question: How do you make navigation links accessible for keyboard users?
intermediate.answer: Ensure :focus styles are visible, use proper tabindex order, and make links focusable with a:focus outline.

intermediate.question: What is the purpose of skip-to-content links?
intermediate.answer: Skip-to-content links allow keyboard users to bypass navigation and jump directly to the main content area.

advanced.question: How do you implement a hamburger menu with CSS-only (no JavaScript)?
advanced.answer: Use a hidden checkbox input and the :checked pseudo-class to toggle a submenu's max-height or transform.

advanced.question: How do you handle sub-navigation depth in multi-level menus?
advanced.answer: Use nested lists with increasing left positioning or transform offsets for each level, with z-index management to prevent overlap.

advanced.question: How do you create a mega-menu navigation?
advanced.answer: A mega-menu uses absolute positioning with columns (CSS columns or Grid) inside a hover-triggered dropdown container.

advanced.question: How do you implement a progress-indicating navigation bar?
advanced.answer: Use JavaScript to track scroll position and adjust active states, or use Intersection Observer to highlight sections as they scroll into view.

advanced.question: How do you make a navigation bar accessible with ARIA attributes?
advanced.answer: Use role='navigation', aria-label, aria-current='page' for active links, and aria-expanded for hamburger toggles.

advanced.question: How do you implement a breadcrumb navigation bar?
advanced.answer: Use an ordered list with ::before pseudo-elements (content: '/') between items, styled with flexbox or inline-block.

advanced.question: How do you create a tab bar navigation similar to mobile apps?
advanced.answer: Use Flexbox with flex: 1 on each item, fixed bottom positioning, and icon + label pairs.

advanced.question: How does sub-pixel rendering affect navigation item widths?
advanced.answer: Sub-pixel rounding can cause the last item to wrap or leave a gap; using flexbox with flex: 1 prevents this.

advanced.question: How do you optimize navigation performance with will-change?
advanced.answer: Use will-change: transform on sticky/fixed navs to promote them to their own compositor layers, reducing repaint cost on scroll.

advanced.question: How do you implement a collapsible sidebar navigation using CSS Grid?
advanced.answer: Use grid-template-columns with a toggleable value (e.g., 250px vs 0) and overflow: hidden on the sidebar container.
